page.content: The first table presents various questionnaires related to haemophilia across different age groups and measures, including the headers Age Group, Type of Measures*, Name of questionnaire, Instrument, Dimension/subscales, N.°of Items**, Way of administration, Reliability, Validity in Haemophilia Examined, N° of languages, and Use in haemophilia Research.

lines.text: The first table presents various questionnaires related to haemophilia across different age groups and measures, including the
headers Age Group, Type of Measures*, Name of questionnaire, Instrument, Dimension/subscales, N.°of Items**, Way of
administration, Reliability, Validity in Haemophilia Examined, N° of languages, and Use in haemophilia Research.

page.content: For Children, the HRQoL measure Haemo-QoL encompasses a haemophilia-specific QoL questionnaire for children patients and parents with dimensions/subscales covering 12 aspects of life, consisting of 21 items for ages 4-7, 64 for ages 8-12, and 77 for adolescents aged 13-16, administered via Self/Proxy, achieving a reliability of α = 0.85-0.91, with validity confirmed in haemophilia, available in 40 languages, and used in various research contexts. For Adults, the Haem-A-QoL questionnaire includes 10 dimensions, 46 items, and a reliability of α = 0.74-0.88, available in 32 languages. The questionnaire Medtap (Haemo-QoL-A) measures 4 dimensions with 41 items and a reliability of α = 0.75-0.95 in 20 languages. The questionnaire Hemo-SatA evaluates treatment satisfaction with 6 dimensions, 34 items, and a reliability of α = 0.71-0.95 in 24 languages, while the HEP-Test Q assesses physical functioning with 5 dimensions, 26 items, and a reliability of α = 0.70-0.90 in 3 languages. The second table details interaction tests, featuring headers Test (interaction), β(SE), and p-Value, reporting results for Serial digit learning (AG × age) with Non-AG at 0.26 (0.17), p = 0.13, and AG at −0.16 (0.12), p = 0.20; Symbol-digit latency (AG × education) with Non-AG at −90.87 (40.17), p = 0.03, and AG at −197.52 (33.12), p < 0.01.

lines.text: For Children, the HRQoL measure Haemo-QoL encompasses a haemophilia-specific QoL questionnaire for children patients
and parents with dimensions/subscales covering 12 aspects of life, consisting of 21 items for ages 4-7, 64 for ages 8-12, and 77
for adolescents aged 13-16, administered via Self/Proxy, achieving a reliability of α = 0.85-0.91, with validity confirmed in
haemophilia, available in 40 languages, and used in various research contexts. For Adults, the Haem-A-QoL questionnaire
includes 10 dimensions, 46 items, and a reliability of α = 0.74-0.88, available in 32 languages. The questionnaire Medtap
(Haemo-QoL-A) measures 4 dimensions with 41 items and a reliability of α = 0.75-0.95 in 20 languages. The questionnaire
Hemo-SatA evaluates treatment satisfaction with 6 dimensions, 34 items, and a reliability of α = 0.71-0.95 in 24 languages,
while the HEP-Test Q assesses physical functioning with 5 dimensions, 26 items, and a reliability of α = 0.70-0.90 in 3
languages. The second table details interaction tests, featuring headers Test (interaction), β(SE), and p-Value, reporting results
for Serial digit learning (AG × age) with Non-AG at 0.26 (0.17), p = 0.13, and AG at −0.16 (0.12), p = 0.20; Symbol-digit
latency (AG × education) with Non-AG at −90.87 (40.17), p = 0.03, and AG at −197.52 (33.12), p < 0.01.
